title: Story History Shortcut

story.i_want_to: retrieve the full audit trail for a specific story in a single API call
story.so_that: I can understand the complete lifecycle of a story — every status change, assignment, verification, and rejection — without manually filtering the audit log

rationale: Orchestrators frequently need the full history of a single story to make verification decisions, diagnose stalled work, or understand why a story was rejected. Forcing them to query the generic audit endpoint with entity_type and entity_id filters is cumbersome. A dedicated convenience endpoint reduces API call complexity and makes the most common audit query trivial.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/stories/:id/history returns all audit log entries where entity_type='story' and entity_id=:id, ordered by inserted_at ascending (chronological).
The response includes full audit entry details: id, action, actor_type, actor_id, actor_label, old_state, new_state, metadata, inserted_at.
Returns 404 Not Found if the story ID does not exist or does not belong to the authenticated tenant.
The endpoint is accessible to agent role and above (agent, orchestrator, user).
Results are tenant-scoped via RLS. A tenant can only view history for their own stories.
The history also includes audit entries for related entity actions that reference the story (e.g., artifact_report entries where the metadata or entity references this story_id).
The response is paginated with a default page size of 100. Supports page and page_size query parameters.
The story itself is validated to exist before querying the audit log. If the story exists but has no audit entries, an empty array is returned with 200 OK.

test_cases.name: returns full chronological history for a story
preconditions: Multiple audit entries for the story: created, status_changed (assigned), status_changed (implementing), status_changed (reported_done), verified
expected_results: Returns all 5 audit entries in chronological order (inserted_at ascending)
First entry is action='created', last entry is action='verified'
Each entry includes full audit details (action, actor_label, old_state, new_state, inserted_at)

test_cases.name: returns 404 for non-existent story
expected_results: Error message indicates story not found

test_cases.name: tenant isolation — cannot view other tenant's story history
preconditions: Audit entries exist for Tenant B's story
steps: GET /api/v1/stories/<tenant_b_story_id>/history with Tenant A's API key
expected_results: Response is 404 Not Found (story not visible to Tenant A due to RLS)

test_cases.name: agent role can access story history
preconditions: Audit entries exist for the story

test_cases.name: empty history for story with no audit entries
preconditions: fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) — inserted directly without triggering audit logging

test_cases.name: pagination works correctly
preconditions: 15 audit log entries exist for the story
expected_results: All entries maintain chronological order across pages

technical_notes: This is a convenience endpoint — it queries the same audit_log table as US-3.1 with a pre-applied filter on entity_type='story' and entity_id=:id.
Route: GET /api/v1/stories/:id/history. Can be added to the existing StoryController or a dedicated StoryHistoryController.
First validate the story exists (and belongs to the tenant via RLS), then query audit_log. This prevents information leakage about story existence across tenants.

For related entries (AC-9.3.6), also include audit entries where metadata contains the story_id (e.g., artifact_report and verification_result entries that reference this story). Use a jsonb query or OR clause on entity_id.
Reuses Loopctl.Audit.list_entries/2 with pre-set filters, or add a dedicated Audit.story_history/2 function.
Ordering is ascending (chronological) — opposite of the main audit endpoint which is descending (most recent first). This makes the history read like a timeline.
